skills.description: Updates a contract template (master data): name, guaranteed/minimum/maximum/full-time hours, night/holiday/Saturday/Sunday rates and validity dates. Only supplied fields change; hour values must not be negative, minimumHours must not exceed maximumHours and validUntil must not be before validFrom. This does NOT assign a contract to an employee — use assign_contract_by_name or assign_contract_to_client for assignments (create_contract is deprecated). Note that the plannability boundary in the schedule comes from Membership.ValidFrom, not from contract dates. Use get_contract_details first to inspect current values.

parameters.name: contractId
parameters.description: UUID of the contract to update

parameters.name: minimumHours
parameters.description: New minimum hours (must not exceed maximumHours)

parameters.name: clearValidUntil
parameters.description: If true, removes the validity end date (open-ended contract)

skills.name: delete_contract
skills.description: Soft-deletes a contract template after verifying it exists. Existing client-contract assignments are not touched by this skill — check with list_contracts / get_contract_details before removal and prefer setting validUntil via update_contract if the contract is still referenced. Use list_contracts to find the contract ID.

parameters.name: contractId
parameters.description: UUID of the contract to delete

skills.name: list_client_availabilities
skills.description: Lists client availability entries (per client, date and hour granularity) for a date range. Each entry says whether a client declared themselves available or unavailable for a specific hour on a specific day. Use this before planning or placing work to respect employee availability; entries are written with set_client_availability. Returns clientId, date, hour and isAvailable per entry.

skills.name: set_client_availability
skills.description: Sets a client's availability (available/unavailable) per day and hour. The backend upserts on (clientId, date, hour), so calling again for the same slots overwrites the previous value. Accepts either an explicit comma-separated list of single days ('dates') or a date range (startDate/endDate, max 92 days), plus an optional hour window (startHour/endHour, defaults to the whole day 0-23). Use search_employees or get_client_details first to resolve the clientId, and list_client_availabilities to inspect the current state.

parameters.name: dates
parameters.description: Optional comma-separated single days in ISO yyyy-MM-dd (e.g. '2026-06-15, 2026-06-17'); takes precedence over startDate/endDate.

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Range start in ISO yyyy-MM-dd; required when 'dates' is not given.

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: Optional range end in ISO yyyy-MM-dd; defaults to startDate (single day).

parameters.name: startHour
parameters.description: Optional first hour of day (0-23); defaults to 0.

parameters.name: endHour
parameters.description: Optional last hour of day (0-23, inclusive); defaults to 23.

parameters.name: isAvailable
parameters.description: true = mark as available, false = mark as unavailable.

skills.name: list_expenses
skills.description: Lists all expense entries in the system. Each expense belongs to a Work entry (shift assignment) and carries amount, free-text description and the taxable flag. Use this to find expense IDs before update_expense / delete_expense, or to give an overview of recorded expenses.

skills.name: add_expense
skills.description: Adds an expense entry (amount, description, taxable flag) to an existing Work entry (shift assignment). The handler enforces day-lock rules and triggers period-hour recalculation plus schedule notifications. Use read_schedule_state to find the workId of the assignment the expense belongs to.

parameters.name: workId
parameters.description: UUID of the Work entry (shift assignment) the expense belongs to.

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Optional free-text description of the expense (e.g. 'Taxi to client site').

parameters.name: taxable
parameters.description: Whether the expense is taxable; defaults to false.

skills.name: update_expense
skills.description: Updates an existing expense entry. Only supplied fields change (amount, description, taxable); fields not supplied keep their current value. Day-lock rules are enforced and period-hour recalculation runs. Use list_expenses first to find the expenseId.

parameters.name: expenseId
parameters.description: UUID of the expense entry to update.

skills.name: delete_expense
skills.description: Soft-deletes an expense entry. Day-lock rules are enforced and period-hour recalculation plus schedule notifications run automatically. Inverse of add_expense for rollback purposes. Use list_expenses first to find the expenseId.

parameters.name: expenseId
parameters.description: UUID of the expense entry to delete.

skills.name: get_autonomy_level
skills.description: Returns the user's current autonomy level for Klacksy: 0 Propose (every write needs confirmation), 1 Assisted (reversible and scenario-gated actions run directly), 2 Autonomous (everything except sensitive actions runs directly, default), 3 FullyAutonomous (plans also run through without per-step approval; sensitive actions still need confirmation). Use this when the user asks what Klacksy may do on its own or why a confirmation was requested.

en: why do you ask for confirmation

skills.name: set_autonomy_level
skills.description: Sets the user's autonomy level for Klacksy. Level 0 Propose: Klacksy only suggests, every write needs explicit confirmation. Level 1 Assisted: reversible and scenario-gated actions run directly, irreversible ones need confirmation. Level 2 Autonomous (default): everything except sensitive actions (user administration, permission changes, autonomy changes) runs directly. Level 3 FullyAutonomous: agent plans also execute non-reversible steps without per-step approval; sensitive actions always keep requiring confirmation. Changing the level is itself a sensitive action and always asks the user to confirm. Use this when the user says Klacksy should work more autonomously or should always ask first.

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: The new autonomy level: 0-3 or one of Propose, Assisted, Autonomous, FullyAutonomous

skills.name: get_dashboard_summary
skills.description: Returns the dashboard key figures for the current month as a compact summary: per-group shift coverage (total slots, covered slots, open slots, coverage ratio) and sealing progress (work entries with LockLevel >= Confirmed vs. total), plus overall totals across all visible groups. Groups are ordered worst-covered first so attention goes to problem areas. Use this when the user asks 'how does the dashboard look', 'how is coverage this month', 'how many open slots do we have' or 'how far is sealing/confirmation'. No parameters needed; the period is always the current month. For the year-long capacity-vs-demand analysis (understaffed/tight days per month, critical months) use interpret_resource_monitor instead; for the employee location map use get_client_locations_overview.

skills.name: get_client_locations_overview
skills.description: Summarizes the dashboard location map as compact counts instead of a raw client list: how many active clients have a current address, breakdown by client type (employee / external / customer), counts per country, the most frequent cities (top N) and how many entries are geocoded (have coordinates). Respects the calling user's group visibility (non-admins only see clients of their visible groups). Use this when the user asks 'where do our employees live', 'how are clients distributed geographically', 'which cities do most people come from' or about the dashboard map. Optionally filter by clientType. For per-client geographic group assignment use the customer-grouping skills; for coverage figures use get_dashboard_summary.

parameters.name: topCities
parameters.description: Maximum number of cities to return, default 10 (capped at 50)

skills.name: list_sealed_orders
skills.description: Lists sealed orders (shifts with Status=SealedOrder) that are eligible for the order export, including customer name/number and Work-closing statistics (totalWorks vs closedWorks, readyForExport flag). Use this when the user asks which orders can be exported, whether an order is complete enough to export, or before open_order_export to identify the right order. The export itself is order-based (SealedOrder selection with recursive descendant resolver), so this list is the authoritative source of exportable units. Related skills: open_order_export (start the download in the UI), list_recent_exports (history of completed exports), close_period (close works so an order becomes fully exportable).

parameters.name: fromDate
parameters.description: ISO date yyyy-MM-dd, lower bound of the order active window.

parameters.name: untilDate
parameters.description: ISO date yyyy-MM-dd, upper bound of the order active window.

parameters.name: customerId
parameters.description: UUID of the customer the orders belong to.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Case-insensitive search across order abbreviation, order name and customer name/number.

skills.name: list_recent_exports
skills.description: Lists completed order export runs from the export log (newest first): format, exported data period, file name and size, record count, who exported and when. The date range filters on the exported DATA period (StartDate/EndDate overlap), not on the execution timestamp; defaults to the last 365 days when omitted. Use this when the user asks whether/when an export was already done, who exported a period, or which file was produced. Note: the log stores metadata only — the file itself was downloaded by the browser at export time and cannot be re-downloaded from the log; to re-run an export use open_order_export. Related skills: list_sealed_orders (what can be exported), open_order_export (trigger a new export in the UI).

parameters.name: fromDate
parameters.description: ISO date yyyy-MM-dd, lower bound of the exported data period. Defaults to 365 days before untilDate.

parameters.name: untilDate
parameters.description: ISO date yyyy-MM-dd, upper bound of the exported data period. Defaults to today.

skills.name: open_order_export
skills.description: Opens the period-closing page with the order export card so the user can download the export file. The export file (CSV/JSON/XML/DATEV/BMD) is generated server-side but streamed as a direct browser download, so the chat cannot deliver the file itself — this skill returns a navigate UiAction and the user picks the sealed order and format in the UI, which also writes the export-log entry. Pass format when the user named one; unsupported formats (e.g. Excel) fail early in the chat with the list of supported keys. Use after list_sealed_orders confirmed which order to export. Related skills: list_sealed_orders (find/verify the order), list_recent_exports (check it was logged afterwards), close_period (close remaining works first if the order is not fully closed).

skills.name: list_email_folders
skills.description: Lists all email inbox folders (system folders like INBOX, Sent, Trash plus user-created folders) with their unread and total email counts. Use this first when the user asks about the mailbox structure or how many unread emails exist per folder, and to obtain the imapFolderName values that the folder parameter of list_emails expects. Read-only: it never creates, renames or deletes folders. Related skills: list_emails (list emails of one folder), read_email (read a single email).

skills.name: list_emails
skills.description: Lists received emails from the mail inbox, newest first, as a compact overview per email: ID, sender, subject, received date, read flag and attachment flag — body content is never included. Filter by folder (IMAP folder name from list_email_folders), limit the result with maxResults (1-50, default 20) and restrict to unread emails with unreadOnly. Also returns the total and unread counts of the selected scope. Use this when the user asks what is in the inbox, whether new or unread emails arrived, or to find an email ID before read_email. Read-only: it does not mark emails as read and cannot move, send or delete emails.

parameters.name: folder
parameters.description: IMAP folder name to filter by (use list_email_folders to find it); omit for all folders.

parameters.name: maxResults
parameters.description: Maximum number of emails to return (1-50).

parameters.name: unreadOnly
parameters.description: If true, only unread emails are returned.

skills.name: read_email
skills.description: Reads a single received email by its ID and returns subject, sender, recipient, received date, folder, read flag and the plain-text body. HTML-only emails are stripped to readable text and very long bodies are truncated (bodyTruncated flag is set). Use list_emails first to find the email ID. Privacy-aware and strictly read-only: attachment contents are never returned (only a has-attachments flag), the email is NOT marked as read, and there is deliberately no skill for sending, moving or deleting emails. Related skills: list_email_folders, list_emails.

parameters.name: emailId
parameters.description: UUID of the email to read (from list_emails).

skills.name: list_transcription_dictionary_entries
skills.description: Lists the entries of the multilingual transcription dictionary that powers the fuzzy phonetic speech-to-text correction. Each entry returns its id, the correct term, an optional category, the known phonetic variants (common STT mishearings), an optional description and an optional language code that selects the phonetic encoder. Use this to review which terms get corrected during voice transcription and to find entry IDs before calling update_transcription_dictionary_entry or delete_transcription_dictionary_entry. Supports optional filtering by language code and by substring of the correct term. For the configured STT/TTS engines themselves use get_speech_settings.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Optional case-insensitive substring filter on the correct term.

skills.name: add_transcription_dictionary_entry
skills.description: Adds a new entry to the multilingual transcription dictionary so the speech-to-text pipeline corrects misheard words to the correct term (e.g. STT hears 'Klaxi' and writes 'Klacksy'). Phonetic variants are optional: the fuzzy phonetic matcher also catches unlisted sound-alike words via the per-language phonetic encoder, but listing known mishearings makes the correction deterministic. phoneticVariants accepts a comma/semicolon separated string or a JSON array. The optional language code (e.g. 'de') selects the phonetic encoder for fuzzy matching; without it the utterance locale is used. The dictionary cache is invalidated automatically, so the change applies to the next transcription. See list_transcription_dictionary_entries for an overview and update/delete_transcription_dictionary_entry for maintenance.

parameters.name: correctTerm
parameters.description: The correct spelling of the term.

parameters.name: phoneticVariants
parameters.description: Optional known mishearings, comma/semicolon separated or as JSON array.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Optional category grouping (e.g. product, person, place).

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Optional short description of the term.

parameters.name: language
parameters.description: Optional language code (e.g. 'de') selecting the phonetic encoder.

fr: ajouter un terme au dictionnaire de transcription
it: aggiungere un termine al dizionario di trascrizione

skills.name: update_transcription_dictionary_entry
skills.description: Updates an existing transcription dictionary entry. Only supplied fields change: correctTerm must stay non-empty, a supplied phoneticVariants value (comma/semicolon separated or JSON array) replaces the existing variant list, and an empty string clears category, description or language. The dictionary cache is invalidated automatically so the change applies to the next transcription. Use list_transcription_dictionary_entries first to find the entryId. Related: add_transcription_dictionary_entry, delete_transcription_dictionary_entry.

parameters.name: entryId
parameters.description: UUID of the dictionary entry to update.

parameters.name: correctTerm
parameters.description: Optional new correct spelling; must not be empty when supplied.

parameters.name: phoneticVariants
parameters.description: Optional replacement variant list, comma/semicolon separated or as JSON array.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Optional new category; empty string clears it.

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Optional new description; empty string clears it.

parameters.name: language
parameters.description: Optional new language code (e.g. 'de'); empty string clears it.

fr: mettre à jour un terme du dictionnaire

skills.name: delete_transcription_dictionary_entry
skills.description: Deletes an entry from the transcription dictionary so its term is no longer corrected during speech-to-text transcription (neither via listed variants nor via fuzzy phonetic matching for that term). The dictionary cache is invalidated automatically. Use list_transcription_dictionary_entries first to find the entryId. Related: add_transcription_dictionary_entry, update_transcription_dictionary_entry.

parameters.name: entryId

skills.name: get_speech_settings
skills.description: Reads the current voice/speech configuration from the settings store: the active speech-to-text engine (browser, deepgram, groq-whisper, assemblyai), the LLM model and enabled-flag used for transcription enhancement, the assistant output mode, the text-to-speech provider and voice, and the silence threshold in milliseconds. For each STT API key it returns only whether a key is configured (HasSttApiKey, HasDeepgramApiKey, HasGroqApiKey, HasAssemblyAiApiKey), never the key itself. Custom STT providers (own table, not settings) and the transcription prompt text are not included. Use this before discussing or diagnosing voice input/output behaviour; for the phonetic correction terms use list_transcription_dictionary_entries.

skills.name: list_languages
skills.description: Lists the active UI languages (with fallback order) and all known language packs: the core languages de/en/fr/it plus optional plugin packs (e.g. Swiss German 'gsw', Romansh 'rm') with installation state, version, translation coverage, translation count and text direction. Use this when the user asks which languages Klacks supports, which language packs are installed, or to find the pack code before install_language_pack / uninstall_language_pack. Read-only. Not related to machine translation of texts — for the DeepL translation service state use get_translation_status.

skills.name: get_translation_status
skills.description: Reports whether the DeepL machine-translation service is configured with an API key, together with the core target languages (de, en, fr, it). Use this when the user asks why automatic translation of multilingual text fields does not work, or before recommending translation features. If the service is not configured, point the user to update_deepl_settings to store the API key (do not duplicate that skill). Read-only; it does not validate the key against the DeepL API. Not related to UI language packs — see list_languages for those.

skills.name: install_language_pack
skills.description: Installs (activates) a locally available optional language pack by its code (e.g. 'gsw' for Swiss German). Activation stores the installed-language setting and imports the pack's translations, skill and navigation synonyms, sentiment keywords, wake words, geo data and documentation. Fails for unknown codes, for core languages (de/en/fr/it are always active) and for packs requiring a newer Klacks version. It does NOT download new packs from the marketplace — only packs already present on the server can be activated. Use list_languages first to find available pack codes; reversible at any time via uninstall_language_pack. Admin only.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: Language pack code to install, e.g. 'gsw' or 'rm' (see list_languages)

skills.name: uninstall_language_pack
skills.description: Uninstalls (deactivates) an installed optional language pack by its code. Removes the pack's imported synonyms, sentiment keywords, geo data and documentation and disables the language in the UI; the pack files remain on disk, so install_language_pack can re-activate the pack at any time. Core languages (de/en/fr/it) cannot be removed; the skill also rejects unknown or not-installed codes. Use list_languages to see which packs are currently installed. Admin only.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: Language pack code to uninstall, e.g. 'gsw' or 'rm' (see list_languages)

Use this to discover valid country abbreviations before filtering states with list_states, when validating or completing address data, or when the user asks which countries are available. Read-only reference data; to look up a Swiss city or postal code use lookup_location instead.

skills.name: list_states

Use this when the user asks which cantons/states exist, to validate canton abbreviations in address workflows, or before canton-based filtering (e.g. the canton parameter of list_contracts). Use list_countries first to discover valid country abbreviations; to resolve a city or postal code to its canton use lookup_location instead. Read-only reference data.

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: Optional country abbreviation (e.g. 'CH', 'DE', 'AT') to return only states of that country. Case-insensitive. Omit to list all states of all countries.

skills.name: list_identity_providers
skills.description: Lists all configured identity providers (LDAP, Active Directory, OAuth2, OpenID Connect) used for single sign-on and client import. Returns only non-sensitive fields per provider: id, name, type, enabled flag, sort order, whether it is used for authentication and/or client import, plus the last sync time/count/error. Bind passwords, client secrets and other credentials are NEVER included. Use this skill first to find the provider id before calling update_identity_provider or delete_identity_provider, or to answer questions like 'which SSO providers are configured' or 'is LDAP login enabled'. Read-only and safe; requires admin rights because the controller is admin-only.

skills.name: create_identity_provider
skills.description: Creates a new identity provider for single sign-on or client import (types: Ldap, ActiveDirectory, OAuth2, OpenIdConnect). Secret parameters (bindPassword, clientSecret) are accepted and stored, but they are NEVER echoed back — responses only carry boolean presence flags (HasBindPassword/HasClientSecret). The provider is created disabled unless isEnabled is explicitly true, so the configuration can be reviewed first. Changing identity providers can lock users out — double-check with the user. This skill is classified as sensitive and always requires user confirmation before execution. Related skills: list_identity_providers to verify the result, update_identity_provider to adjust or enable it later, delete_identity_provider to remove it.

parameters.name: isEnabled
parameters.description: Whether the provider is active. Defaults to false so the configuration can be reviewed before going live.

parameters.name: useForAuthentication
parameters.description: Whether users can log in through this provider.

parameters.name: useForClientImport
parameters.description: Whether clients are imported/synced from this provider.

parameters.name: port
parameters.description: LDAP/AD server port (1-65535), e.g. 389 or 636 for SSL.

parameters.name: useSsl
parameters.description: Whether the LDAP/AD connection uses SSL.

parameters.name: bindPassword
parameters.description: Secret. LDAP bind password; stored but masked in all responses.

parameters.name: clientSecret
parameters.description: Secret. OAuth2/OIDC client secret; stored but masked in all responses.

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant id for multi-tenant providers (e.g. Entra ID).

skills.name: update_identity_provider
skills.description: Updates an existing identity provider (LDAP, Active Directory, OAuth2, OpenID Connect). Only supplied fields change; everything else keeps its current value. Secret parameters (bindPassword, clientSecret) replace the stored secret only when supplied and are NEVER echoed back — responses carry boolean presence flags instead. Also used to enable/disable a provider (isEnabled) or to toggle useForAuthentication/useForClientImport. Use list_identity_providers first to find the provider id. Changing identity providers can lock users out — double-check with the user, especially when disabling a provider, changing its type, host or credentials. This skill is classified as sensitive and always requires user confirmation before execution. Related skills: list_identity_providers, create_identity_provider, delete_identity_provider.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: UUID of the identity provider to update (find it via list_identity_providers).

parameters.name: useForAuthentication
parameters.description: Whether users can log in through this provider.

parameters.name: useForClientImport
parameters.description: Whether clients are imported/synced from this provider.

parameters.name: useSsl
parameters.description: Whether the LDAP/AD connection uses SSL.

parameters.name: bindPassword
parameters.description: Secret. New LDAP bind password; replaces the stored one only when supplied, masked in all responses.

parameters.name: clientSecret
parameters.description: Secret. New OAuth2/OIDC client secret; replaces the stored one only when supplied, masked in all responses.

skills.name: delete_identity_provider
skills.description: Soft-deletes an identity provider (LDAP, Active Directory, OAuth2, OpenID Connect). Users who authenticate through the deleted provider lose their login path immediately, and client import/sync from that provider stops. Use list_identity_providers first to find the provider id and to check whether the provider is enabled and used for authentication. Changing identity providers can lock users out — double-check with the user. This skill is classified as sensitive and always requires user confirmation before execution. The response contains only non-sensitive fields and warns explicitly when the deleted provider was used for authentication. Related skills: list_identity_providers, create_identity_provider, update_identity_provider (consider disabling via isEnabled=false instead of deleting).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: UUID of the identity provider to delete (find it via list_identity_providers).

skills.name: confirm_pending_action
skills.description: Executes a pending action the user just confirmed. When a skill call is held by the autonomy gate, the action is stored under a one-time confirmation token and NOT executed. After the user explicitly confirmed in their own words, call this skill with that token — the stored action is then executed exactly as originally requested (same skill, same parameters). Never call this without the user's explicit confirmation; never invent or reuse tokens. If the token is expired, trigger the original action again to get a fresh confirmation request.

parameters.name: confirmation_token
parameters.description: The one-time token from the confirmation request of the held action
